summary: Tested leaderboard movement tracking and store credit at checkout. Backend: 17/17 tests pass. Frontend: Store credit toggle works visually but leaderboard movement indicators are MISSING from the UI (the frontend interface and renderItem function don't display movement field at all). data-testid attributes are not accessible via CSS selectors (React Native Web issue).

minor.issue: Cancelling an order does NOT restore store credit (creditBalance) to the user. Only stock is restored. If user applies $X store credit then cancels, they lose that credit permanently. May be intentional but worth flagging.

ui_bugs.issue: CRITICAL: The 'movement' field is NOT in the LeaderboardEntry TypeScript interface and is NOT rendered anywhere in the UI. Backend returns movement=+1/-1/0/null but frontend never displays any movement arrows or indicators. The entire movement tracking feature is invisible to users.
ui_bugs.fix: Add 'movement: number | null' to LeaderboardEntry interface. Add rendering logic in renderItem() to show arrows (↑ green for positive, ↓ red for negative, — neutral for 0).
ui_bugs.backend_data_verified: Justin K. movement=+1, Brianna C. movement=-1, Andrew M. movement=0 - all correct from API

ui_bugs.issue: data-testid attributes on TouchableOpacity are NOT rendered in the DOM (0 data-testid elements found via [data-testid] selector across entire app). React Native Web does NOT forward data-testid from TouchableOpacity/View to the underlying DOM element. All test automation using [data-testid='store-credit-toggle'] etc. will fail.
ui_bugs.fix: Change data-testid to testID on React Native TouchableOpacity and View components. testID is the React Native prop that gets forwarded to data-testid in the web DOM.

leaderboard_movement_backend.details: GET /api/leaderboard returns 'movement' field for each user. Justin K. movement=+1 (rank 2→1), Brianna C. movement=-1 (rank 1→2), Andrew M. movement=0 (same rank). byReferrals also has movement field. Snapshot loop running correctly.

leaderboard_movement_frontend.details: Movement arrows/indicators are COMPLETELY ABSENT from the leaderboard UI. The LeaderboardEntry TypeScript interface in leaderboard.tsx does not include 'movement' field. renderItem() function has no code to display movement.

leaderboard_snapshot_exists.details: Yesterday's snapshot (2026-03-09) exists with swapped rankings. Today's snapshot (2026-03-10) also exists. Movement values confirmed as non-null for top users.

store_credit_toggle_visible.details: Apply Store Credit toggle visible in checkout page with '$12.00 available' text. Admin user creditBalance > 0.

store_credit_order_summary.details: When toggled, Order Summary shows 'Store Credit -$12.00' row, Total updates from $25.00 to $13.00, Place Order button updates to 'Place Order - $13.00'.

store_credit_deduction.details: Backend test verified: placing order with storeCreditApplied=$1.00 deducts $1.00 from creditBalance. credit_before - credit_to_apply = credit_after verified.

store_credit_capped_at_balance.details: Backend test verified: requesting more credit than available is capped at available balance. Frontend correctly caps at min(availableCredit, preTotalBeforeCredit).

store_credit_in_order_document.details: storeCreditApplied field exists in Order schema and is correctly stored. GET /api/orders/{id} returns storeCreditApplied.

action_items: CRITICAL: Add 'movement' field to LeaderboardEntry interface in leaderboard.tsx and implement movement arrow display in renderItem(). Backend returns correct data (+1/-1/0/null) but frontend completely ignores it.

MEDIUM: Fix data-testid attribute propagation - change data-testid to testID on React Native TouchableOpacity/View components across the app. Currently 0 data-testid elements in the DOM, making CSS-based test automation impossible.
MINOR/REVIEW: POST /api/orders/{id}/cancel does not restore creditBalance when store credit was applied to the cancelled order. Verify if this is intentional or a bug.
critical_code_review_comments: leaderboard.tsx line 12-20: LeaderboardEntry interface missing 'movement: number | null' field
leaderboard.tsx line 58-91: renderItem() has no movement indicator rendering (no arrow icons, no color coding)
All .tsx components: data-testid on TouchableOpacity/View doesn't reach DOM - should use testID prop
order_routes.py cancel_order(): No creditBalance restoration when storeCreditApplied > 0 and order is cancelled - potential credit loss on cancellation

success_rate.frontend: 60% (store credit UI works, leaderboard movement UI completely missing)

test_credentials.note: Credit balance was $20 at session start, backend tests deducted some during testing

seed_data_creation: No seed data created. Backend tests placed Zelle orders (Pending Payment status) and cancelled them to restore stock.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests all pass. Frontend leaderboard movement display is entirely missing - the main agent needs to add movement field to the interface and rendering. Store credit UI works correctly (toggle visible, order summary updates, totals correct). The data-testid attribute issue is systemic - no data-testid CSS selectors work anywhere in the app because React Native Web doesn't forward data-testid from TouchableOpacity. Use text-based selectors for Playwright automation (e.g., 'text=Apply Store Credit'). Admin creditBalance at test time was $12.00.

leaderboard_movement_missing_from_ui.root_cause: The LeaderboardEntry TypeScript interface in frontend/app/leaderboard.tsx was not updated when the backend added the 'movement' field to the leaderboard response. The renderItem() function has no code to display movement direction indicators.
leaderboard_movement_missing_from_ui.reproduction: Navigate to /leaderboard while logged in. No movement arrows are visible. API returns movement=1 for rank 1 user but UI shows nothing.
leaderboard_movement_missing_from_ui.mitigation: Add movement: number | null to LeaderboardEntry interface. Add conditional rendering in renderItem() to show ↑ (green) for positive, ↓ (red) for negative, or nothing for null/0.

data_testid_not_in_dom.root_cause: React Native Web doesn't forward data-* attributes from custom components like TouchableOpacity/View to the underlying DOM elements. The web standard is testID -> data-testid but data-testid prop is not a React Native prop.
data_testid_not_in_dom.mitigation: Use testID prop instead of data-testid on all React Native interactive components. Example: testID='store-credit-toggle' instead of data-testid='store-credit-toggle'.
